circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track designed by Hermann Tilke on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island, has served as the venue for Formula One's season finale since 2009. The circuit represents the second of four Middle Eastern Formula One venues, following Bahrain's establishment and preceding later additions in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: A street circuit located around Albert Park Lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb, this FIA Grade 1 venue hosts the Formula One Australian Grand Prix each year alongside the Supercars Championship Melbourne SuperSprint and various supporting races.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit was originally established as Österreichring and served as the Austrian Grand Prix venue for 18 years during the 1970s and 1980s. Following renovations and a name change to the A1-Ring, the track resumed hosting the Austrian Grand Prix from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street racing venue in Azerbaijan's capital, situated along the Baku Boulevard waterfront, with a 6. 003 km layout that ranks as the fourth-longest track in Formula One. The venue first hosted the 2016 European Grand Prix before being renamed the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards, with operations managed by the Baku City Circuit Operation Company.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit spans 5. 412 km and hosted the Middle East's inaugural Formula One Grand Prix that year. Beyond Formula One, the venue has accommodated various motorsport events including GP2 Series competitions, Australian V8 Supercars racing (2006-2010), and 24-hour endurance races, while maintaining FIA Grade 1 certification with multiple configurable track layouts.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced blend of lengthy straightaways and diverse corner configurations, making it a comprehensive test of driver and vehicle capability. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: This Belgian circuit near Spa spans 7. 004 km and has been hosting Formula One racing since 1950, appearing in the sport's inaugural season. The venue has maintained continuous Grand Prix status from 1985 onwards, missing only the 2003 and 2006 editions, and currently serves as the home of the Belgian Grand Prix.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, Quebec, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361 km urban track that serves as the home of the Formula One Canadian Grand Prix. Beyond Formula One, the circuit has hosted various major motorsport series including world sportscar racing, open-wheel championships, and multiple NASCAR competitions throughout its history.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: Shanghai International Circuit, located in Shanghai's Jiading District, is a motorsport venue renowned for hosting the Chinese Grand Prix, which took place annually from 2004 to 2019 before resuming in 2024. The track also began hosting the Shanghai ePrix in 2024, expanding its calendar beyond Formula One racing.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Silverstone, located in England near Northamptonshire, is the traditional venue for the British Grand Prix, which it hosted continuously from 1948 and notably staged the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. Though the British Grand Prix alternated between three circuits from 1955 to 1986, it has been held exclusively at Silverstone since 1987, which also regularly features MotoGP racing.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring near Budapest was constructed in just eight months and hosted the first Formula One Grand Prix held in Eastern Europe when it opened in 1986. Located in Mogyoród outside the Hungarian capital, this 4. 381 km circuit was built as an alternative to a proposed street course in Budapest's main park after government preference favored a dedicated racing facility near a major highway.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Monza in northern Italy, the 5. 793 km Autodromo Nazionale is Europe's oldest purpose-built racing circuit, established in 1922. It has continuously hosted the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, earning its nickname "Temple of Speed" for its exceptionally fast layout featuring extended straights and high-speed turns.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, operated by Honda Mobilityland in Mie Prefecture, Japan, spans 5. 807 kilometers and hosts major motorsport events including Formula One and Super Formula championships. The venue accommodates up to 155,000 spectators and remains one of the most prestigious racing destinations in the world.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a temporary street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, alongside the famous Las Vegas Boulevard. The layout passes by iconic casino resorts and entertainment venues such as Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, as well as the MSG Sphere entertainment venue.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the 4. 304 km Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is named after two brothers who tragically died racing, Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, with the track receiving its current name in 1979 following their respective fatal accidents. The venue has hosted the Formula One Mexican Grand Prix on multiple occasions since 2015, resuming after a 23-year absence from the championship calendar, with the race now officially called the Mexico City Grand Prix from 2021 onwards.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: The Miami International Autodrome is a temporary street circuit built around Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, measuring 3. 363 miles with 19 turns and an average qualifying speed of approximately 139 mph. Designed specifically for Formula One by specialist circuit architects, the venue hosts the Miami Grand Prix, which joined the championship calendar in 2022.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco stretches 3. 337 km through the streets of Monte Carlo and La Condamine, winding around Monaco's harbour. Though officially named after the principality itself, the track is widely known as Monte Carlo since most of its layout occupies that neighbourhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, this 4. 259 km track hosted the Dutch Grand Prix after rejoining the Formula One schedule in 2021 following a decades-long absence. The venue will conclude its current Formula One partnership after the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: Lusail International Circuit, situated north of Doha near the city of Lusail, is a 5. 419-kilometre racing venue featuring a 1. 068-kilometre main straight and an FIA Grade 1 classification.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km Brazilian racing circuit in São Paulo that opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace following his fatal plane crash. The counterclockwise track also features a kart facility dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street racing venue in Saudi Arabia positioned along the Red Sea waterfront. Since 2021, it has served as the home of the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix, and it began hosting an international electric racing championship event in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's downtown waterfront district, incorporating the Downtown Core and Kallang planning areas as it traces a path around Marina Bay.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: The Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya is a 4. 657 km Spanish racing venue located in Montmeló near Barcelona, featuring a versatile layout with extended straights and diverse corner configurations that test multiple racing disciplines. Holding an FIA Grade 1 license, the track accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that serves as the permanent home of the Formula One United States Grand Prix. Beyond Formula One, the track hosts major international motorsport events including NASCAR races, MotoGP's Motorcycle Grand Prix of the Americas, and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai and British racing driver who currently races for Williams in Formula One, competing under the Thai flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso represents Aston Martin in Formula One, bringing two world championship titles earned with Renault in 2005 and 2006 plus 32 Grand Prix victories across his 23-season career. Beyond Formula One, Alonso has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans 24 Hours victories with Toyota, the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, and the 2019 Daytona 24 Hours, making him the sole driver to win both the Formula One and World Endurance championships.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli made his Formula One debut in 2025 with Mercedes, where he continues to race. He has secured five Grand Prix victories during his time in the sport.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for the Haas team.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian racing driver Gabi Bortoleto competes in Formula One representing Audi, following a career progression through various motorsport categories.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas has secured two runner-up finishes in the World Drivers' Championship, both achieved during his tenure with Mercedes in 2019 and 2020. Currently competing for Cadillac in Formula One, Bottas has accumulated 10 Grand Prix victories throughout his 13-season racing career.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Argentine driver Franco Alejandro Colapinto currently races in Formula One for the Alpine team. He represents his country in the sport's premier motorsport competition.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan combines endurance racing with his Formula One commitments, competing in the European Le Mans Series while holding a reserve role at Haas. He has made appearances at seven Formula One events between 2024 and 2025.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: French driver Pierre Gasly currently races for Alpine in Formula One, having previously secured a victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix during his time with AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar, a driver holding both French and Algerian nationality, currently races for Red Bull Racing in Formula One under the French flag.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari, holding an equal-record seven world championship titles alongside Michael Schumacher. He also leads the sport in multiple statistical categories, including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races for Audi in Formula One. Beyond his single-seater commitments, he achieved endurance racing success by winning the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 alongside Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand motorsport competitor who currently drives for Racing Bulls in the Formula One championship.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc, a driver from Monaco, currently races for Ferrari in Formula One. He finished as championship runner-up in 2022 and has secured nine Grand Prix victories throughout his nine seasons in the sport.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver who has competed in Formula One across two separate periods, most recently concluding his tenure in 2024. Currently, he focuses on endurance racing with BMW's WRT team in the World Endurance Championship while also pursuing part-time NASCAR commitments with Trackhouse Racing.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver competing for McLaren, secured the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship with the team. Across his eight seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon, a French racing driver, currently competes in Formula One for Haas. He previously achieved a race victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Sergio Pérez represents Mexico in Formula One, currently racing for Cadillac. The experienced driver finished second in the 2023 world championship while with Red Bull and has secured six Grand Prix victories throughout his 15-season career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri, an Australian driver competing for McLaren in Formula One, has claimed victory in nine Grands Prix over the span of four seasons.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Australian driver Daniel Ricciardo competed in Formula One over a 14-year span from 2011 through 2024, securing eight Grand Prix victories during his career. Known by the nickname "the Honey Badger," Ricciardo has since retired from the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, having secured seven race victories throughout his eight seasons competing in the championship.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, where he has claimed four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver who raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to endurance racing. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category for Proton Competition, having previously raced in the IMSA SportsCar Championship's LMP2 class.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian driver currently competing in Formula One, where he races for Aston Martin.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Japanese driver Yuki Tsunoda has held a reserve role with Red Bull Racing and its sister team Racing Bulls in Formula One, having previously competed in the championship as a full-time participant between 2021 and 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: Dutch driver Max Verstappen has secured four consecutive world championships between 2021 and 2024 while representing Red Bull Racing, accumulating 71 race victories throughout his 12-year Formula One career.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu holds the distinction of being China's sole Formula One competitor to date, having raced in the sport from 2022 through 2024. He currently fulfills a reserve driver role for the Cadillac Formula One team.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: The Enstone-based Alpine F1 team has operated as Renault's works operation since rebranding from its previous iteration in 2021 to showcase the French carmaker's sports car division. Powered by Mercedes engines and competing under a French licence, the squad secured its only victory in its Alpine identity when Esteban Ocon triumphed at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, before Renault's departure from the sport concluded the partnership at the end of 2025.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has competed in Formula One through different organizational structures as a British manufacturer, with its current incarnation operating as AMR GP Limited under the Aston Martin Aramco branding.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, stands as Formula One's longest-running and most accomplished outfit, participating continuously since the championship's inception in 1950. The squad, colloquially dubbed the Prancing Horse after its emblem, remains the sport's most successful team by multiple measures.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas founded this American F1 team in 2014, which commenced racing in the 2016 season. Ayao Komatsu assumed the role of team principal in 2024, succeeding Guenther Steiner who led the operation since its establishment.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Founded by Peter Sauber in 1970, this Swiss engineering firm established itself through endurance racing success, including a Le Mans victory and two world championships before joining Formula One in 1993. The team operated as an independent constructor for over three decades, becoming one of the sport's longest-serving teams before ceasing operations in 2025, when its assets were acquired by Audi to serve as the foundation for the manufacturer's F1 programme.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren is a British Formula One team headquartered in Woking, Surrey, operating as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group. The outfit competes in the sport under corporate sponsorship and is wholly owned by Bahraini investment firm Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has been a major presence in Formula One since 1954, functioning as both a constructor and engine supplier across multiple eras. The current iteration, Mercedes-AMG Petronas, operates from Brackley under a German licence and became jointly owned by Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and team principal Toto Wolff from January 2022. The marque's racing teams are traditionally known as the "Silver Arrows".

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that entered the sport in 2024, based in Faenza with an additional facility in Milton Keynes. As one of two constructors owned by Red Bull GmbH, it operates as a sister team to Red Bull Racing within the Austrian conglomerate's motorsport portfolio.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team that commenced operations in 2024 as a constructor and competitor. Owned by Red Bull GmbH alongside the senior Red Bull Racing outfit, the Faenza-based squad operates with a secondary facility near its sister team's Milton Keynes headquarters.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, operating under an Austrian license despite being based in England, emerged as a Formula One competitor in 2005 and evolved into one of two series entries controlled by Red Bull GmbH. Christian Horner steered the team from its inception through early 2025, when he was succeeded by Laurent Mekies.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, succeeding Williams's earlier failed racing operation. Based in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team competes in 2026 under the Atlassian Williams F1 Team name and operates from a substantial facility spanning 60 acres.
